hypothesis: the current remote architecture is globally singleton-scoped and production is already showing remote-stream fragility
action: initialized execution tracker and captured production baseline health, remote status, and session inventory into docs/execution/remote-browser-lease-rollout/artifacts/baseline/
expected_signal: baseline artifacts exist and confirm current remote control state before any edits
result: tracker created; baseline health, remote status, and session inventory artifacts were saved successfully
decision: use the baseline artifacts as the before-state proof for the backend replacement and later production comparison

hypothesis: the lease cutover is locally correct if the full backend suite and the frontend test/build pipeline pass from the current worktree
action: ran `pytest` in backend and `npm run test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` in frontend while teeing outputs into docs/execution/remote-browser-lease-rollout/artifacts/local/
expected_signal: backend passes without lease regressions; frontend passes tests/build and only shows pre-existing lint warnings
result: backend passed with 327 tests green; frontend tests passed, build passed, and lint completed with only the existing `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` warnings in App.tsx
decision: promote the current worktree to deployment candidacy and use the saved outputs as the local verification proof set
playbook_note: save command outputs under task artifacts so deploy decisions are anchored to files, not terminal scrollback

hypothesis: even without a local proxy, the new remote-control stack should expose stable api state and show a clear operator-facing error instead of hanging
action: ran local api smoke against `/health`, `/sessions`, `/sessions/remote/status`, and `/sessions/FB_Android_1/remote/start`, then opened the local frontend sessions tab and launched the remote modal for `FB_Android_1`
expected_signal: session payloads include reservation metadata; remote status reports capacity 2; remote start fails with an explicit proxy error that the modal surfaces visibly
result: the local api returned reservation metadata and a capacity limit of 2, the remote start call failed with `no proxy available...`, and the frontend remote modal displayed the same failure while transitioning to a disconnected state
decision: treat missing local proxy as an environment blocker, not a product regression, and rely on production for full interaction-quality proof
playbook_note: when local browser launch is blocked by environment prerequisites, prove the explicit error path through api output and the operator ui rather than guessing

hypothesis: the backend can ship first without breaking the existing production frontend if the old websocket/http contract is still honored by the new lease service
action: pushed backend commit `0c5b03c`, waited for railway deployment success, authenticated into the current production frontend, opened remote control from the old sessions UI, and sent a legacy scroll interaction
expected_signal: railway serves the intended commit, the old frontend still boots a remote session, and a legacy action reaches the new backend
result: railway deployed commit `0c5b03ce4b03cbdaae0ac71136b8773dc1e8cc79`; the old frontend opened remote control for `adele_compton`, rendered a facebook frame, and logged a successful `scroll down` action before the session was explicitly stopped
decision: the backend-first rollout is safe; proceed to the frontend deployment on top of the live lease service
playbook_note: for skew-sensitive rollouts, prove the old client against the new server with at least one real action, not just a successful connection

hypothesis: the cleanup can safely remove the rollout shim if dead websocket sends are pruned consistently and closed-socket runtime errors are treated as disconnects
action: patched backend/remote_lease_service.py and backend/main.py to use prune-on-failure viewer sends, remove legacy remote action translation, and classify closed websocket runtime errors as disconnects; deleted backend/browser_manager.py; reran focused regressions, the full backend suite, the frontend test/lint/build pipeline, and a local runtime smoke against /health, /sessions, /sessions/remote/status, and /sessions/FB_Android_1/remote/start
expected_signal: the cleanup leaves one remote engine, no singleton file, no legacy websocket action shim, green local verification, and normal local runtime behavior without disconnect log spam
result: focused remote regressions passed, backend finished at 333 passed, frontend tests/lint/build stayed green, and a live local uvicorn smoke returned healthy /health and /sessions/remote/status responses plus the expected explicit missing-proxy remote-start error
decision: promote the cleanup worktree to the final deployment candidate and verify the fixed disconnect path plus strict action contract in production
playbook_note: when a websocket-backed control loop can see starlette closed-socket runtime errors, classify them as disconnects and break the loop; otherwise a dead client can spin error logs and mask the real session state

hypothesis: the final cleanup deployment is production-safe if railway/vercel serve the intended commits, capacity and reservation rules hold live, takeover works with the new controller surviving a peer disconnect, and recent logs stay free of closed-websocket spam
action: verified railway commit 8a6f8f3 and vercel deployment dpl_CvTB2NLLifm4iiAX19JLfLPr2Ee3, rechecked production health/status, ran a live capacity+reservation smoke across facebook and reddit detached starts, ran a two-user websocket takeover/disconnect proof on adele_compton, filtered recent railway logs for the previous closed-websocket signatures, and opened the production frontend sessions tab to launch a live facebook remote modal
expected_signal: prod health is healthy, remote status returns to zero active leases, capacity rejects the third lease with 409, refresh is blocked with 409 on a leased profile, takeover succeeds, a post-disconnect controller action still succeeds, no recent closed-websocket spam appears in logs, and the production frontend renders a live remote frame
result: railway and vercel both served the intended production state; capacity and reservation checks returned the expected 200/409 responses; the websocket proof showed controller attach, observer attach, successful takeover to ollietr, successful scroll after the original controller disconnected, zero active leases after explicit stop, and healthy post-stop status; recent railway logs contained zero matches for the prior closed-websocket spam strings; the production frontend sessions UI opened a live facebook remote modal and rendered a connected frame
decision: the lease cutover is fully verified end-to-end in production; commit the proof artifacts and close the task with no open rollout or legacy-code work remaining
playbook_note: after a remote modal closes, expect the lease to persist at viewer_count=0 until idle timeout or explicit stop; production verifiers should check that detached state explicitly instead of assuming immediate lease deletion

hypothesis: the remaining production failures come from two distinct defects: the ui can reconnect after modal close, and the current cdp pixel path can produce blank facebook frames even when the lease is otherwise ready
action: reproduced the live issue on production by probing the remote status endpoint, attaching fresh websocket clients to Vanessa Hines and Wanda Lobb, saving the first delivered frame images, and extracting the earlier railway log window around the false-capacity incident
expected_signal: if the hypothesis is right, production should show fresh leases that report browser_ready yet deliver blank frame images, and the earlier stop window should show a browser start immediately after a user-initiated stop or close path
result: fresh production websocket attaches for both facebook profiles delivered blank white 393x873 jpeg frames despite valid lease_role, state, and browser_ready events; the saved railway log window shows manual_stop for Vanessa and Wanda followed by a new browser_start for Vanessa, matching an unintended reconnect or reopen path rather than a true cleared stop
decision: patch the frontend close lifecycle to disable reconnect before socket close and explicitly stop sole-controller leases, then replace the backend cdp frame transport with a page-screenshot frame pump and re-verify on production

hypothesis: the lifecycle hotfix is locally safe if the final worktree passes the full backend suite plus the remote-control frontend test/build gate without introducing any new lint warnings
action: ran `pytest` in backend and `npm run test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` in frontend after the reconnect, pagehide, wheel, and renderability patches landed in commit f65b815
expected_signal: backend stays fully green, frontend remote-control tests stay green, build passes, and lint shows no new warnings outside the pre-existing App.tsx list
result: backend finished at 336 passed; frontend finished at 14 passed across 5 files; frontend build passed; lint stayed at 7 pre-existing App.tsx warnings only
decision: promote commit f65b815 to production verification and treat the remaining risk as production-only behavior, not local correctness
playbook_note: for websocket-ui lifecycle fixes, capture a minimal local proof set that separates genuine regressions from pre-existing lint debt before spending time on production

hypothesis: the hotfix is truly done only if production proves three things simultaneously: vanessa no longer promotes a blank shell, leaving/reloading during startup no longer leaks capacity, and reddit control remains healthy
action: verified vercel success for commit f65b815, refreshed the production ui onto bundle index-BB8jDaND.js, opened Vanessa Hines remote control in the Sessions tab, waited through session-proxy failure into env fallback, closed the modal, re-opened Vanessa and reloaded the page during startup to test pagehide stop, then opened and closed reddit_amy_schaefera from the Reddit tab while saving status/log artifacts
expected_signal: vanessa stays in starting until a real renderable page arrives, modal close and page reload both return remote status to zero active leases, and reddit still reaches a live frame on first attempt and closes cleanly
result: production matched the expected behavior: vanessa only became browser_ready after env fallback with title Facebook and htmlLength 23287, explicit close returned remote status to zero active leases, page reload during startup logged manual_stop plus browser_start_cancelled and left zero active leases, reddit_amy_schaefera reached browser_ready on the first session-proxy attempt with a live frame, and closing the reddit modal again returned zero active leases
decision: accept the lifecycle hotfix as production-complete and keep the live behavior artifacts as the authoritative proof, noting only that railway cli commit reporting lagged during the proof window
playbook_note: for browser-control regressions, production proof should explicitly cover ready-path quality, close-path release, and reload-during-startup behavior, not just a successful attach
